title: Iran Ryad

[[Countess/Legends|Countess]] '''Iran Ryad''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Politics/Legends|politician]] and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Ace/Legends|ace]]. A career in politics during the reign of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] saw her become a wealthy and influential individual who could afford to take up starfighter [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] training as a hobby.

After the loss of many skilled [[TIE pilot|Imperial pilots]] during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Ryad gave up her political career to become a pilot. She used her influence to requisition an advanced [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE/D Defender]] as her [[Red Star 1|personal starfighter]] and eventually gained command of [[Red Star Squadron]]. Under Ryad's command, the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] began attacking any [[Starship/Legends|ships]] that ventured into Imperial [[Realspace/Legends|space]] unannounced. After Ryad was accused of [[Treason/Legends|treason]], she was hunted down and [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the Imperial ace [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]].

==Biography==
===Galactic Civil War===
A [[Human/Legends|Human]], [[Countess/Legends|Countess]] Iran Ryad forged a career as a [[Politics/Legends|politician]] in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], giving her a position of wealth and influence. Throughout her life, she developed a fascination not only for military politics, but also for the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. The latter obsession encouraged Ryad to seek training as a starfighter [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], a hobby enabled by her political connections and prodigious wealth.<ref name="Wizards" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
Sometime following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Iran Ryad partook in a skirmish against at least one [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]]. Ryad was defeated in the fight, but managed to escape alive in her crippled [[TIE Oppressor]].<ref name="SWG" />
{{Endgame}}
Around [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the Empire developed the [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE/D Defender]],<ref name="3ABY">In [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']], the [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defender]] enters service between the battles of [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] and [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Endor]], placing it in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> a radical departure for the [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] which featured many systems absent from most Imperial starfighters, including both [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] and a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. The high cost of the starfighter, together with its departure from standard Imperial tactics, made it unpopular and Ryad was one of the few experienced and influential politicians to argue in favor of the new starfighter.<ref name="Wizards" />

[[File:TIEdefender_negvv.png|thumb|right|220px|A TIE Defender, which Ryad supported the development of]]
In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Galactic Empire suffered a major defeat at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] at the hands of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] himself was [[Death/Legends|killed]], as was his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Furthermore, the Imperial Navy suffered heavy losses, including the destruction of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]]<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> and many of its most skilled [[TIE pilot]]s.<ref name="Wizards" /> The death of Palpatine saw the Empire begin to fragment, with rival military figures declaring themselves [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] and attempting to establish their own dominions.<ref name="NEC" />

===After Endor and death===
With the Empire running short of skilled pilots, Ryad abandoned her career as a politician and became an Imperial pilot, in time utilizing her connections to requisition a TIE Defender to serve as her personal starfighter, the ''[[Red Star 1]]''. Ryad had the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] modified<ref name="Wizards" /> at a cost of 62,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]],<ref name="Cost">''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]'' lists the cost of the ''Red Star 1'' as 262,500 credits, 62,500 credits higher than that of an unmodified TIE Defender.</ref> fitting it with an enhanced [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]], an improved [[Ion engine/Legends|ion drive]] for greater maneuverability and advanced [[Targeting computer/Legends|fire control]] systems.<ref name="Wizards" />

[[File:CountessRyadTieDefender.png|thumb|left|200px|Ryad's fighter, the ''Red Star 1'', was a modified TIE Defender.]]
The Countess spent over a year leading a [[Flight group/Legends|flight group]] of four TIE Defenders on patrols of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Coreward]] borders of what remained of Imperial [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. After a dozen successful patrols, Ryad had scored a high number of kills and became a starfighter [[Ace/Legends|ace]]. Her achievements resulted in her gaining command of her own [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]]. Ryad filled [[Red Star Squadron]] with like-minded pilots who shared her idea of a zero-tolerance border policy. With Ryad leading the squadron from the ''Red Star 1'', the Imperial pilots relentlessly pursued any starships that entered Imperial space without permission.<ref name="Wizards" />

Throughout her career, Ryad had gained rivals within the Empire, foremost among them being another notable Imperial pilot, [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]],<ref name="Wizards" /> a veteran of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] who had remained loyal to the Empire under the rule of [[Ysanne Isard]], the former [[Director of Imperial Intelligence|Director]] of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] who served as the steward of the Empire following Palpatine's death.<ref name="NEGC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> Around [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],<ref name="Death" /> Ryad's actions led her to be accused of [[Treason/Legends|treason]] and she was hunted down and killed by Fel.<ref name="Wizards" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:FelinROTJ.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Iran Ryad's unconventional beliefs led to the countess being killed by Baron Soontir Fel (pictured).]]
Iran Ryad was an eccentric and influential politician and diplomat with a long-held interest in the military and [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] history. She was not afraid to use either her wealth or connections for personal gain. She used her influence to have herself trained as a starfighter pilot and later to acquire her personal TIE Defender. Although piloting was initially merely a hobby for her, she devoted herself to it and became a skilled pilot and commander who could inspire confidence from those serving under her. Ryad went against conventional ideas on military tactics, favoring the use of the TIE Defender over large waves of unshielded starfighters and advocating a strict border policy in which any unannounced intruders were destroyed. Her beliefs, however, had a high cost and ultimately led to her death.<ref name="Wizards" />

Ryad had [[Color/Legends|blonde]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], blue [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and fair skin.<ref name="SWG" />

==Equipment==
Iran Ryad wore a black [[Imperial Navy crewman|Imperial Navy crewwoman's]] [[Imperial military uniforms/Legends|uniform]].<ref name="SWG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Iran Ryad was created by [[Brian Campbell]] and [[Owen K.C. Stephens]] for the ''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]'' web supplement [[Starships of the Galaxy|''More Starships!'']], which was published on [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s [[Wizards.com|website]] on [[November 15]], [[2001]].<ref name="Wizards" />

In the [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|MMORPG]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', Iran Ryad was added to the [[Star Wars video games|game]] as a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player character]] (NPC) in [[W:c:swg:Game Update 20|Game Update 20]]. Rebel players who requested [[W:c:swg:Tier|Tier 7]] [[W:c:swg:Destroy Duty Missions|Duty Missions]] from certain [[Space station/Legends|space stations]] had a chance to battle an [[W:c:swg:Ace Pilot System|elite Imperial pilot]] during the mission's boss stage. One of these pilots could be Iran Ryad. If the player defeated Ryad, the Imperial was not destroyed. Instead, a message was displayed to the player, indicating that they successfully defeated the pilot. The player then received a [[W:c:swg:Collection|collection]] icon which added up to award a [[W:c:swg:Badges|badge]] if they succeeded in destroying all ten of the elite Imperial pilots. However, elite Imperial pilots could also be found flying in various space battles, which took place in the [[Naboo system/Legends|Naboo]], [[Tatoo system/Legends|Tatoo]], and [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system]]. Because Ryad only appeared in the space portion of ''Star Wars Galaxies'', originally added with the [[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed|''Jump to Lightspeed'']] expansion pack, she could normally only be seen inside her respective TIE Oppressor. However, a comm display could appear during the battle, displaying Ryad's face. In addition, the collection icon depicted Ryad's physical appearance.<ref name="SWG" />
